result.lens: Equivariant topology of configuration spaces: flex monodromy of unit-bar frameworks, discretized into twisted orbit graphs whose 5-coloring count is a holonomy trace.
result.first_principles:
Ask where R^2 differs from all three controls simultaneously. (1) Realization topology: the realization space of a unit-bar framework mod isometry is positive-dimensional and path-connected in pieces over R^2 (rhombus 4-bars flex; loops exist), but is totally disconnected over Q^2 and discrete over R^1. Flexes and their monodromy exist ONLY in the Euclidean plane among the four. (2) Circle rigidity: two Euclidean unit circles meet in at most 2 points, so along a flex the wall events (an extra pair hitting distance exactly 1) are isolated and exactly computable; in L^infty, unit circles intersect in segments, so wall values are not isolated and orbit-closure arithmetic degenerates. (3) Color-phase equivariance: the bipartite controls Q^2 and R^1 carry a canonical 2-coloring phase preserved by every isometry, so every geometrically realized loop automatically has consistent coloring holonomy; R^2 at k=5 has no canonical 5-phase, so nothing forces realized loops to be holonomy-consistent. All three differences point at one object: a loop in the realization variety of a flexible framework, sampled at N points with cross-slice unit edges, killed by inconsistent coloring holonomy.

Naive flex monodromy proves nothing because colorings are discrete per embedding and an arbitrary plane coloring is not continuous along the flex. The repair is purely combinatorial: close the discretized loop and count. Number of proper 5-colorings of the twisted orbit graph = tr(T^N P_sigma), where T is the slice transfer relation on interface colorings and sigma is the twist from flex monodromy or dihedral closure. If T is block-cyclic of period p (a Z/p flux on S_5-invariant color patterns; single-vertex ports are excluded by the L55 monoid, so the interface has width >= 2, exactly route (ii)) and geometry selects N, s with p not dividing N+s, the trace vanishes identically: chi >= 6. The lineage cannot contain this: its graphs are rigid-cluster rotation orbits with trivial monodromy, retrodicting L57's total freeness. Moser's spindle is the k=3 degenerate case: rhombus flex, two wall values, single-port holonomy.

conjectures.name: C1: Twisted flex-orbit trace obstruction (coloring flux quantization)
conjectures.statement: Define a slice as a finite UDG S with exact realization rho and two disjoint m-tuples I_0, I_1 of vertices (m >= 2) such that some g in SE(2) maps rho(I_0) to rho(I_1) pointwise. Let X be the set of restrictions to I_0 of proper 5-colorings of S, and T subset X x X the transfer relation (x,y) in T iff some proper 5-coloring restricts to x on I_0 and y on I_1. Theorem part (elementary trace algebra, conditional on realizability): if there exists f: X -> Z/p with p >= 2 such that every (x,y) in T has f(y) = f(x) + 1 mod p, and g has finite order N about a center O with an optional interface symmetry twist sigma satisfying f(x compose sigma) = f(x) + s, then whenever p does not divide N + s, the orbit graph union_{j=0}^{N-1} g^j(rho(S)) (with interface identifications, plus any coincidental unit edges) has NO proper 5-coloring, hence chi(R^2) >= 6. Existence conjecture: such a slice exists with chi(S) = 5, m <= 4, p = 2, at most 150 vertices, built from two de Grey H-cluster fragments bridged by a pair of unit-rhombus 4-bars, with f a parity-type invariant of the interface color pattern.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: There exists a UDG, findable by SAT-guided search over Q(sqrt3, sqrt11) orbit constructions in this repo, containing two disjoint non-adjacent vertex pairs (a1,a2), (b1,b2) such that proper 5-colorings realize the pattern combinations (same, diff) and (diff, same) but NEITHER (same, same) NOR (diff, diff). Refuted by a proof that every realizable slice has primitive transfer at k=5, or by exhaustive failure over a declared finite family of rhombus-bridged H-cluster slices.
conjectures.why_plausible: The abstract object exists (the repo's 48-vertex abstract clamp yields a pattern-flipper by gluing one pendant edge, all SAT-checkable); L55 proves route (ii) is the only live route and this is a concrete instantiation of it; the trace mechanism is sound algebra; and the lineage's exhaustive freeness (L57) is explained by rigidity rather than impossibility, since every lineage graph is a rigid rotation orbit with trivial monodromy.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Realizability could impose an S_5-equivariant completeness law at the pattern level, a wide-interface analog of the L55 single-port monoid, forcing every UDG-realizable slice with free marginals to have complete pattern transfer. Also, if de Grey's omega<=3 rival world is right and chi(R^2)=5, every such search fails for the deepest possible reason.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The certificate schema chi>=6 iff p does not divide N+s, with the twist sourced from flex monodromy of a flexible sub-framework, is to my knowledge unstated anywhere. Nearest prior art: de Grey/Polymath16 spindling and virtual edges (k=4 forcing through single-vertex ports) and this repo's L55 imprimitivity diagnostic; none has the twisted-closure trace formula or a geometric origin for imprimitivity.

conjectures.name: C2: Rigid-orbit completeness (the structural cause of L57)
conjectures.statement: For every graph in the 12-graph chi-5 lineage and every two disjoint non-adjacent pairs (a1,a2), (b1,b2): all four joint pattern combinations in {same, diff} x {same, diff} are realized by proper 5-colorings (marginal feasibility is already guaranteed by L57 freeness). Equivalently, conditioning on c(a1) = c(a2) by merging, or on c(a1) != c(a2) by an added edge, creates ZERO new forced pairs anywhere in the graph. Structural form: UDGs assembled from generically rigid clusters glued along rotation orbits have complete pairwise pattern transfer at k = 5; long-range chromatic correlation requires a flexible sub-assembly.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: The conditional forcing sweep on S199 (the experiment below) returns zero conditional forcing quadruples across all conditioning pairs. A single quadruple ((a1,a2) merged forces (b1,b2) same or different) kills the conjecture.
conjectures.why_plausible: L57's perfect uniformity over ~2.29M pairs is too clean to be coincidence and demands a structural law; the natural law is that the rigid-orbit grammar of the lineage produces only adjacency-local forcing, with Kempe-chain recolorings acting transitively on joint patterns because rigid clusters contribute independent color degrees of freedom.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Joint distributions can be constrained even when all marginals are free; L45/L56/L57 only ever tested unary pair forcing, so 2-pair correlations are genuinely unexplored. If a quadruple exists, C2 dies, and that failure is valuable: it would be the first k=5 forcing object of any kind found inside the lineage and immediate raw material for C1's block-cyclic slice.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The lineage sweeps stopped at pairs; conditional (quadruple) forcing has never been tested, and the proposed link from generic rigidity of the bar framework to completeness of chromatic transfer couples rigidity theory and chromatic forcing, which I believe is absent from the entire Hadwiger-Nelson literature.

conjectures.name: C3: Coloring cosheaf and wall-crossing monodromy on the realization variety
conjectures.statement: For a flexible unit-bar framework (G, rho_0), let M be its realization space mod orientation-preserving isometries, stratified by walls W_{uv} = {rho : |rho(u) - rho(v)| = 1, uv not an edge}. Assign to each rho the finite set C(rho) of proper 5-colorings of the coincidence-closed unit-distance graph on rho(V); this is locally constant on open strata, and wall incidences give inclusion correspondences. Claim 1: this defines a constructible correspondence-valued local system on M, and for any loop gamma transverse to walls at rho_1, ..., rho_r with deck permutation sigma in Aut(G) (the flex monodromy), the finite UDG formed as the union of the wall configurations rho_i(V) has zero proper 5-colorings whenever the twisted composite correspondence C(rho_1) -> ... -> C(rho_r) -> (via sigma) C(rho_1) has zero trace. Claim 2 (exact calibration): at k = 3 the unit-rhombus 4-bar reproduces the Moser spindle: the flex angle has exactly two wall values where the apex pair reaches distance 1 (two-circle rigidity), and the 2-wall composite trace at k = 3 is zero, recovering chi(spindle) = 4. Claim 3 (frontier): there exists a loop with r <= 4 wall configurations and fixed-point-free twisted holonomy at k = 5, yielding a chi >= 6 UDG with under 1000 vertices.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Claim 2 is a finite exact computation: sympy solve for the rhombus flex angles with apex distance 1 (must yield exactly 2 solutions), then brute-force 3-coloring correspondences and verify the composite trace is 0 and equals the spindle's 3-coloring count. If the formalism fails to retrodict Moser at k=3, it is wrong. Claim 3 is falsified if all flex loops of rhombus-chain frameworks up to a declared size have a holonomy fixed point at k=5.
conjectures.why_plausible: Moser spindling IS the r = 2, k = 3 case of this formalism in disguise, and every known HN lower bound (de Grey included) was produced by spindling moves; the formalism names the structure that already generated all lower bounds and converts the k=5 question into one about pi_1 of realization varieties, where Euclidean R^2 is provably richer than all three controls.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The composition of wall correspondences may lose information: the honest union UDG contains ALL cross-copy unit coincidences, not only the engineered ones, so its coloring set can be strictly smaller than what the composite trace counts. Then nonzero trace would not certify colorability and the formalism keeps only the useful one-way blade (zero trace implies chi >= 6) while the local-system structure of Claim 1 needs weakening to a lax 2-functor.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Treating proper colorings as a constructible correspondence system on the realization variety, with wall-crossing holonomy around flex loops as the lower-bound engine, is stated nowhere I know of. Nearest art: de Grey spindling, Polymath16 virtual edges, and Connelly-style rigidity configuration-space theory, which has never been coupled to coloring.

experiment.description: Conditional forcing sweep on S199 (decides C2, and either opens or closes lineage mining for C1). Adapt shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py: load S199 via its load_graph(); for each non-adjacent conditioning pair (a1,a2), merge a1=a2 using the existing rep() merge from forced_different(); in the conditioned graph, enumerate ~8 proper 5-colorings with cadical (randomized phases plus blocking clauses), take the candidate set of pairs colored differently in ALL samples (minus adjacency) and pairs colored same in ALL samples, then verify each surviving candidate with one decisive UNSAT call (merge for forced-different, added edge for forced-same). Record any quadruple ((a1,a2) | (b1,b2), type). Zero hits over all conditioning pairs confirms C2 on S199 and proves holonomy material must be built outside the lineage from flexible assemblies; any hit kills C2, is the first k=5 forcing object ever found in the lineage, and directly supplies the off-diagonal block of C1's pattern-cyclic slice. Second-stage tie-break (only if hits appear): test the four joint patterns for hit quadruples to check for the full flipper relation (same,same) and (diff,diff) both infeasible.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Pure python-sat (cadical) plus existing infrastructure: c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/experiments/combinatorial/shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py already contains the graph loader, the merge-based forced_different test, the edge-based forced_same test, and checkpointing; the only new code is the coloring-sample intersection pruner (~80 lines). No C compiler, no new dependencies. Cache results under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/shotE/.
experiment.est_cost: S199 has roughly 19k non-adjacent conditioning pairs; per pair about 8-12 incremental solves at 5-30 ms on a 199-vertex instance gives 1-3 hours single-threaded for the full sweep; a 2000-pair random sample gives a decisive first read in about 10 minutes.

detector_self_check.Q2: The final certificate is a finite UDG whose non-5-colorability is SAT-decided, so nothing lifts formally. Mechanistically the firing condition (a geometrically realized loop with inconsistent coloring holonomy) is provably unsatisfiable over Q^2: the rational unit-distance graph is bipartite with a canonical 2-phase preserved by every rational isometry, so any realized closure automatically satisfies p divides N+s (an inconsistent realized loop would create an odd closed walk, contradicting bipartiteness). Moreover Q^2 realization spaces are totally disconnected: there are no flexes, hence no monodromy source. The mechanism cannot prove chi(Q^2) >= 3.
detector_self_check.Linf: The schema is a lifting principle that consumes chi-5 slice gadgets with imprimitive 5-transfer as input; on the L^infty plane chi = 4 exactly, so no chi-5 slice is realizable there and the schema has nothing to lift. Independently, the closure arithmetic needs isolated wall values and exactly pinned interfaces, which rest on Euclidean two-circle rigidity (two unit circles meet in at most 2 points); L^infty unit circles intersect in segments, so wall values are non-isolated and the orbit-closure data (exact N, s) degenerates. It cannot over-prove chi(L^infty) >= 5.
detector_self_check.R1: Unit-bar frameworks on the line have discrete realization spaces mod isometry: no flexes, trivial pi_1, no monodromy to twist with, and R^1's unit graph is bipartite with an isometry-equivariant phase, so the same holonomy-consistency exclusion as Q^2 applies. The mechanism consumes O(2) essentially: the flexes, the order-N rotational closures, and the dihedral twists are all rotation-built, so it is structurally silent on R^1 and is not rotation-blind.
